0.last_action: House Stricken from Calendar by Rule 1507
0.title: Increasing the criminal penalties for buying sexual relations, removing provisions regarding city ordinances prohibiting buying sexual relations, requiring certain offenders to complete an educational or treatment program regarding commercial sexual exploitation and requiring the attorney general to approve such programs in consultation with the office of judicial administration.

1.last_action: House Stricken from Calendar by Rule 1507
1.title: Prohibiting certain sex offenders from entering onto school property or attending school activities and creating criminal penalties for violation thereof.

2.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
2.title: Expanding the definition of stalking to include more technology-based methods, requiring law enforcement agencies to adopt policies for making primary aggressor assessments when responding to domestic violence calls and requiring a prosecutor to affirm such assessment was completed, prohibiting public agencies from charging fees to victims of domestic violence for public records, allowing evidence of other domestic violence offenses to be admissible in a criminal action in which the defendant is accused of a domestic violence offense, adding certain contact to options for protection from abuse or stalking orders and allowing lifetime extensions of protection from abuse orders for victims of domestic battery.

3.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
3.title: Providing that no person shall be sentenced to death for crimes committed after July 1, 2025, and creating the crime of aggravated murder.

4.last_action: House Referred to Committee on Judiciary
4.title: Providing that no person shall be sentenced to death for crimes committed after July 1, 2025, and creating the crime of aggravated murder.

5.last_action: House Referred to Committee on Judiciary
5.title: Increasing the time of initial orders and possible extensions under the protection from abuse act and the protection from stalking, sexual assault or human trafficking act.

6.last_action: House Referred to Committee on Judiciary
6.title: Prohibiting certain sex offenders from residing within 1,000 feet of any school property.

7.last_action: Senate Hearing: Tuesday, March 11, 2025, 10:30 AM Room 346-S
7.title: Prohibiting certain sex offenders from entering onto school property or attending school activities and creating criminal penalties for violation thereof.

8.last_action: Senate Conference Committee Report was adopted; Yea: 36 Nay: 4
8.title: Senate Substitute for HB 2164 by Committee on Judiciary - Prohibiting certain sex offenders from entering onto school property or attending school activities and creating criminal penalties for violation thereof.

9.last_action: House Approved by Governor on Thursday, February 5, 2026
9.title: Changing the culpability required for certain types of theft and increasing the criminal penalty for theft to a felony when the property is a motor vehicle of the value of at least $500.

10.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
10.title: Requiring any person convicted of distribution of a controlled substance causing great bodily harm or death or register under the Kansas offender registration act for 15 years.

11.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
11.title: Requiring offenders convicted of distribution of a controlled substance causing great bodily harm or death to register under the Kansas offender registration act.

12.last_action: Senate Motion to accede adopted; Senator Warren, Senator Titus and Senator Corson appointed as conferees
12.title: Increasing the cumulative detention limit for juvenile offenders and criminal penalties for juvenile offenders who use a firearm in the commission of an offense or who are repeat offenders, providing for increased placement of offenders in non-foster home beds in youth residential facilities, requiring the secretary of corrections to pay for the costs associated with such placements and authorizing the secretary to make expenditures from the evidence-based programs account of the state general fund moneys to contract for such beds.

13.last_action: House Emergency Final Action - Passed as amended; Yea: 124 Nay: 0
13.title: Changing the membership of the prisoner review board and requiring parole hearings to be postponed if proper notice of the public comment session is not made to the victim.

14.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
14.title: Requiring veterans with a condition from military service to be offered diversion for certain offenses and expanding the mitigating factor for departure at sentencing for veterans.

15.last_action: House Referred to Committee on Judiciary
15.title: Adding consideration of whether the offender has physical custody of such offender's minor child or is a legal guardian or custodian with physical custody of a minor child to the factors considered for diversions and dispositional departures.

16.last_action: Senate Committee of the Whole - Be passed as amended
16.title: Prohibiting courts from assigning a defendant to work release if the defendant is required to register as a sex offender for certain crimes and prohibiting the secretary of corrections from granting an inmate leave from confinement except for specified purposes if the inmate is required to register as a sex offender for certain crimes or is serving a sentence for certain crimes that require registration as a sex offender.

17.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
17.title: Requiring certain records and files to be automatically expunged from a juvenile's record.

18.last_action: House Referred to Committee on Child Welfare and Foster Care
18.title: Authorizing victims of childhood abuse to access records related to substantiated reports or investigations of abuse or neglect and extending the time to file civil actions for recovery of damages caused by childhood sexual abuse.

19.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Judiciary
19.title: Prohibiting fines and fees from being assessed against a juvenile or a juvenile's parent, guardian or custodian in a case pursuant to the revised Kansas juvenile justice code.

20.last_action: House Stricken from Calendar by Rule 1507
20.title: Prohibiting fines and fees from being assessed against a juvenile or a juvenile's parent, guardian or custodian in a case pursuant to the revised Kansas juvenile justice code.

21.last_action: Senate Final Action - Passed; Yea: 25 Nay: 15
21.title: Authorizing the supreme court to impose a charge to fund the costs of non-judicial personnel through June 30, 2030.

22.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Federal and State Affairs
22.title: Removing the criminal penalties for possession of a personal-use quantity of marijuana and creating a civil penalty for possession of a personal-use quantity of marijuana.

23.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Federal and State Affairs
23.title: Enacting the Kansas medical cannabis act to authorize the cultivation, processing, distribution, sale and use of medical cannabis and medical cannabis products.

24.last_action: House Withdrawn from Committee on Federal and State Affairs; Referred to Committee on Interstate Cooperation
24.title: Enacting the Kansas medical cannabis act to authorize the cultivation, processing, distribution, sale and use of medical cannabis and medical cannabis products.

25.last_action: Senate Referred to Committee on Ways and Means
